fullText:
没想到小红书模型训的也可以啊，我一直以为他们只做一些 Agent 方面的事情。

刚才看到他们在 2026 年的国际奥林匹克数学竞赛（IMO）上，那个 dots-note-3.0以 42 分的满分成绩获得了满分金牌的认证，超过金牌线 13 分。这应该是第一次有模型在IMO官方评卷里拿到满分。全球范围内都是。

IMO 主要是证明题，没有选择题之类的对错题，要看论证过程是否准确严密，需要评委去评审。模型在某一个题目上用了非常新颖的解法。

我查了一下，这个模型是dots-note-3.0 的内部版本，应该是 dots 3 中最轻量的模型，近期也会开源，感觉他们在模型上也发力了。

刚好昨天，Anthropic 的数学家用 Fble 5 证明了雅可比猜想的反例，这也说明大模型在数学发现上的重要性越来越强，而且能力上也越来越强了。

fullText:
MY 4 TAKE-AWAYS FROM WAIC 2026

1. Robots and AI are converging rapidly.

Robotics and artificial intelligence are converging rapidly into unified physical and digital systems. 

I experienced this firsthand on the exhibition floor where advanced robotics sat directly above the main AI halls in Shanghai.

2. There is a convergence of world models / building, video generation and 3D model generation

They are merging into connected activities and platforms. 

This convergence enables AI to understand 3D physical environments rather than just processing flat text and imagery.

3. Tencent has a very rapid product cadence. “Choose Your Buddy” is a user-friendly approach.

Tencent is using its revamped AI process for fast product deployment speed into its ecosystem.

Plus, the focus is open source and very low cost. 

Chinese open weights models like Kimi K3 deliver near frontier capability at fifty cents per million tokens compared to fifty dollars for traditional proprietary offerings.

4. We are seeing lots creativity and combinatorial innovation. A Cambrian explosion.

For more, listen to my podcast.

#TechStrategy #WAIC2026 #RoboticsAndAI

https://t.co/gXITgYKELZ

fullText:
Math Refresher for Adults — The Perfect Solution for Mastering Essential Math Skills: https://t.co/8Ly8NGHWzW

There are lots of reasons why you might want to have this book, no matter what your math proficiency might currently be. See the list of reasons on the book cover. 

My reason:
I tutored students in math when I was in high school and college, I took lots of math courses (including honors math classes for math majors) in college, I learned high-level math (group theory, differential geometry & forms, tensor calculus) from Nobel laureates in theoretical physics classes in grad school at Caltech, I taught math in my computational & data science courses as a university professor, I guided students who feared math into doing and loving calculus (conquering their fears and converting some into math lovers), and I sat for countless hours with my own children through many years of math homework & exam prep in their school years. My latest target audience are my grandkids. A book like this keeps me grounded in these new opportunities to share the love and beauty of math with a new generation.

Math Lovers-Я-us!

user.name: AIGCLINK
user.description: 致力于让每个想拥抱AI的人都能找到适合自己的AI产品，助力企业定制AIGC应用

fullText:
"AI 公司"这种 demo 大家看腻了——一堆 agent 互相开会、彼此吹捧、聊到天荒地老，最后什么都没产出。

Auto-Company：将14 个领域的大佬“招聘”到自己公司，一家 24/7 跑在你自己电脑上的全自主 AI 公司（CMU 的 Zheyuan Kong 做的，1908 star），禁止纯讨论，14 个 agent：每个按一位业界大牛的思维模型来设定——CEO 是贝索斯、CTO 是 Werner Vogels、做反向验证的是芒格、全栈是 DHH、营销是 Seth Godin……底层用 Claude Code（默认）或 Codex CLI 来真执行。

有3个值得关注的亮点：

① 用"强制收敛流"治 agent 的通病——只说不做：
流程在 PROMPT.md 里被硬编码：
   -第 1 轮：头脑风暴，各 agent 提案、排出 Top 3
   -第 2 轮：验证 #1——芒格 pre-mortem + 汤普森市场检验 + 单位经济，逼出一个 GO / NO-GO
   -第 3 轮起：GO 就建仓库、写代码、部署，"只讨论不落地的循环被明令禁止"

② 整个跨周期记忆，就是一个 markdown 文件：
没有向量数据库、没有复杂记忆管理——每轮开始前读、结束前重写 memories/consensus.md，当作交接的"接力棒"。你想干预方向，也只是去改这个文件里的 Next Action，下一轮它立刻转向。野心是"自主公司"，引擎却是一个 bash 死循环 + 一个 md 文件。

③ 它不自己造轮子，是"骑"在成熟 CLI 上：
执行层直接调 Claude Code / Codex CLI，使用现成的文件读写、Bash、Git 能力；再用 launchd（macOS）/ systemd（WSL）做守护进程，崩了自动重启，配熔断、限流退避、共识回滚。

注意安全，它需要全权限跑——底层是 Codex 的 danger-full-access 或 Claude 的 bypassPermissions，操作直接发生在你的真实主机上。守护栏（不删库、不 force push）是写在 CLAUDE.md 里的软约束，不是沙箱。

它真正有意思的地方，是把"多 agent 一直空转"这个人人都担心的问题，用最土的办法解决了：不给它无限开会的权利，这比又一个"AI 员工"概念博眼球，实在得多。
#AutoCompany #AIagent #多agent #ClaudeCode #自主AI #开源

fullText:
The world's largest open model sold out 3 days after launch. WHAT?!
Moonshot paused new Kimi K3 subscriptions because demand exceeded GPU capacity.

That same weekend:
• Xi Jinping publicly backed open-source AI.
• Alibaba teased a 2.4T open-weight Qwen 3.8.
• Axios reported that Washington may ban Chinese AI models.

APIs can be blocked. But open weights are much harder to stop once they're out.

Which means the next bottleneck may be permission: who is still allowed to use these models openly?

user.name: elvis
user.description: Building self-improving AI @dair_ai • Prev: Meta AI | PhD • Learn about AI Agents for FREE here: https://t.co/P5SA9u54xO

fullText:
Progressive disclosure in agents doesn't scale.

And its benefits seems agent harness dependent.

(bookmark this one)

Finally there is a proper study on using agent skills and the effect of progressive disclosure.

Progressive disclosure is the agent skills pattern where you hand an agent a document path and let it decide what to read, from a short description down to specific passages.

Practitioners adopted it fast for book-length tasks, purely on vibes.

Researchers ran it across three agent harnesses and three model families on InfiniteBench.

On a single book:

> the gain is harness-dependent,
> large when the agent navigates raw documents poorly,
> near zero when a strong harness already retrieves on its own.

Scale to many books and raw navigation falls apart while one level of disclosure pulls ahead. A second routing level never helps and sometimes breaks accuracy.

It feels like progressive disclosure buys context, but not intelligence. It is redundant while a strong agent can find the passage itself, and decisive once the corpus is too large to read.

Paper: https://t.co/SUOIhmwD8j

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/1e8RZKs4uX

user.name: DAIR.AI
user.description: Democratizing AI research, education, and technologies. Learn about AI Agents for FREE at https://t.co/HHXg8rryu4

fullText:
Very cool idea to convert memory to skills.

(bookmark it)

Most agent memory systems retrieve past traces as passive context. MSCE turns them into executable skills instead.

The training-free framework organizes agent experience into grounded step traces, reusable procedural policies, and declarative environmental cognition. Evidence-backed policies with positive estimated gain get converted into callable skills that carry their applicability boundaries, verification rules, and reliability estimates. So a skill knows when it applies and how to check itself.

The other piece is reflection-weighted value backfilling, which propagates sparse terminal feedback through dense local self-reflections to produce evidence-calibrated trace values. Those values govern which memory and skills survive. On EvoAgentBench and LoCoMo, MSCE outperforms strong skill-augmented and memory-driven baselines with cross-domain transfer.

The gap between memory-as-context and memory-as-capability seems to be where long-horizon agents actually compound.

Paper: https://t.co/rXNkXqsXDx

Learn to build effective AI agents in our academy: https://t.co/LRnpZN7L4c
